text.title: Generic Patterns
text.summary: Generic functions with constraints, repository interfaces, and generic React components for type-safe reusable code.

section.title: Generic Functions and Interfaces

rule.statement: Constrain type parameters with extends when the generic must satisfy a shape contract.
rule.rationale: Unconstrained generics accept any type, losing type-safety inside the function; constraints make the contract explicit at the call site.

section.reference: Generic Repository interface with typed CRUD methods

rule.statement: Type generic React components with a type parameter on the props interface, not with any.
rule.rationale: Generic props let callers pass a typed items array and render callback, while the component stays unaware of the domain type.

antipattern.description: Using any[] as the items type in a reusable list component.
antipattern.reason: Loses type safety in renderItem — errors like accessing a non-existent property only surface at runtime.
